fixtureSchemas.name: object_tags
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each object_tags fixture contains an objectTag array. Every row identifies one compiled object by display name and then lists one or more object analysis tags to assert for that object.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: objectTag
fields.description: Per-object tag expectation rows.
fields.specification: Each row names one object and includes the object tags that this fixture wants to pin down.

fields.name: object
fields.description: Compiled display name of the object under test.
fields.specification: The runner resolves this name against ps_tagged.objects and compares the rest of the row to that object's derived tags.

fields.name: is_player
fields.description: Object is part of the resolved Player role.
fields.specification: An object has is_player when it is the object named by the Player object, Player synonym, or a member of the Player property in the compiled game. If Player resolves to a property with multiple member objects, every member object has is_player true.

fields.name: is_background
fields.description: Object is part of the resolved Background role.
fields.specification: An object has is_background when it is the object named by the Background object, Background synonym, or a member of the Background property in the compiled game. If Background resolves to a property with multiple member objects on the same collision layer, every member object has is_background true.

fields.name: level_presence
fields.description: Whether the object appears in all, some, or no playable levels.
fields.specification: Message levels are ignored. The value all means there is at least one playable level and the object appears in every playable level. The value some means the object appears in at least one but not every playable level. The value none means the object appears in no playable levels; if a valid source has zero playable levels, every object has level_presence none.

fields.name: created_by_rules
fields.description: A solver-active rule may write this object present in a cell.
fields.specification: An object has created_by_rules when rule object-write analysis finds a solver-active rule that may write that object present in a cell where the left-hand side does not already require that object to be present in the same cell. This is a cell-local write tag: relocation can count as creation in the destination cell, even if the object's total count is preserved.

fields.name: destroyed_by_rules
fields.description: A solver-active rule may write this object absent from a cell.
fields.specification: An object has destroyed_by_rules when rule object-write analysis finds a solver-active rule that may write that object absent from a cell where the left-hand side allows that object to be present in the same cell. This includes explicit absent writes, removing a left-hand side object from its original cell, and overwriting a possible same-layer occupant. This is a cell-local write tag: relocation can count as destruction in the source cell, even if the object's total count is preserved.

fields.name: cosmetic
fields.description: Object is not read by any win condition or non-cosmetic rule.
fields.specification: An object is cosmetic when it is not in any win condition's presence or absence read set, not read by any non-cosmetic rule, and does not share a collision layer with any non-cosmetic object. Player-role objects are always non-cosmetic. Background-role objects are cosmetic if unreferenced.

fields.name: static
fields.description: No solver-active rule can move, create, destroy, or overwrite this object.
fields.specification: An object is static when no solver-active rule writes it present, writes it absent, or assigns or reads its movement state. Same-layer overwrite counts as a write-absent. Player-role objects are never static because player input always assigns them movement.

fields.name: temporary
fields.description: No instance of this object survives to the end of a turn.
fields.specification: An object is temporary when it is absent from initial levels and win conditions, every instance is created by solver-active rules, every creator is followed by an unconditional late cleanup clear, and no creator or clearer is rigid or has again taint.

fields.name: count_invariant
fields.description: No solver-active rule changes the total board count of this object.
fields.specification: An object has count_invariant when no solver-active, object-mutating rule may change the total count of that object across all board cells. Relocation is count-neutral; unbalanced creation, destruction, random writes, uncertain same-layer overwrite, and uncertain explicit erasure are not.

fixtureSchemas.name: rule_tags
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each rule_tags fixture contains a ruleTag array. Every row identifies one compiled rule by source line and exact source text, then asserts selected rule tags inside the row's tags object.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: ruleTag
fields.description: Per-rule tag expectation rows.
fields.specification: Each row identifies exactly one analyzed rule and contains a tags object with the rule tags this fixture wants to assert.

fields.name: line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the rule.
fields.specification: Together with text, this must identify exactly one analyzed rule.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the rule.
fields.specification: The runner requires this text to be compiler-idempotent so the fixture remains stable.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Rule tag expectations for this rule.
fields.specification: Each field in tags is a documented rule tag and is compared with the derived rule tag value.

fields.name: objects_required
fields.description: Concrete objects definitely required present by the rule LHS.
fields.specification: Single-object terms are required. Objects of required aggregates are all tagged as required. Members of an OR property are not individually required because any one member may satisfy the term.

fields.name: objects_matched
fields.description: Concrete objects whose presence may satisfy a positive LHS term.
fields.specification: Includes concrete required objects, OR-property alternatives, and objects in aggregates after resolving synonyms, properties, and aggregates to object names.

fields.name: object_absences_matched
fields.description: Concrete objects whose absence may satisfy a negative LHS term.
fields.specification: For example, no Obstacle records every concrete object in Obstacle.

fields.name: movements_required
fields.description: Concrete object movements definitely required by the rule LHS.
fields.specification: Entries use Object:movement. Single-object movement terms are required. Members of an OR property are not individually required because any one member may satisfy the movement term.

fields.name: movements_matched
fields.description: Concrete object movements whose presence may satisfy a rule LHS movement term.
fields.specification: Entries use Object:movement for every concrete object movement that may satisfy a positive LHS movement term after resolving synonyms, properties, and aggregates to object names.

fields.name: objects_written
fields.description: Concrete objects written present by the rule RHS.
fields.specification: This is cell-local: relocation writes the object at the destination even if total object count is preserved.

fields.name: objects_erased
fields.description: Concrete objects written absent by the rule RHS.
fields.specification: Includes explicit no terms, removal from the original cell, and possible same-layer erasure caused by writing another object on the layer.

fields.name: movements_written
fields.description: Concrete object movements written or cleared by the rule RHS.
fields.specification: Entries use Object:movement. RHS movement terms use the compiled movement name visible to this analysis stage. If an LHS movement requirement is removed while the object remains present in the corresponding RHS cell with no RHS movement, the rule writes Object:stationary.

fields.name: movements_removed
fields.description: Concrete object movements no longer present after the rule RHS.
fields.specification: Entries use Object:movement for concrete LHS movement matches that are not preserved by an identical RHS movement match for the same object in the corresponding RHS cell.

fields.name: cosmetic
fields.description: Rule has no reachable effect on win conditions or non-cosmetic rules.
fields.specification: A rule is non-cosmetic if it carries a non-cosmetic semantic command, its object writes intersect a win-condition read, or its writes may enable any non-cosmetic rule.

fixtureSchemas.name: program_flow
fixtureSchemas.description: Fixtures for whole-program rule wake edges.
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each program_flow fixture asserts the projected wake graph for one source program: which rule writes may enable which other rule reads, plus the rules that queue again.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: wakeEdges
fields.description: Rule-to-rule wake edges.
fields.specification: Each edge means the from rule may make the to rule newly match through object presence, object absence, or movement.

fields.name: from_line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the waking rule.
fields.specification: Together with from_text, identifies the rule whose writes may enable another rule.

fields.name: from_text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the waking rule.
fields.specification: Used with from_line to identify the source rule.

fields.name: to_line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the rule that may become newly matchable.
fields.specification: Together with to_text, identifies the destination rule.

fields.name: to_text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the rule that may become newly matchable.
fields.specification: Used with to_line to identify the destination rule.

fields.name: reasons
fields.description: Why the wake edge exists.
fields.specification: Allowed reasons are object_presence, object_absence, and movement.

fields.name: againRules
fields.description: Rules whose firing queues again.
fields.specification: Consumers that want the worst-case wake set should combine wakeEdges with againRules x all rules.

fields.name: line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the again rule.
fields.specification: Together with text, identifies a rule whose summary commands include again.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the again rule.
fields.specification: Used with line to identify a rule whose summary commands include again.

fixtureSchemas.name: winflow
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each winflow fixture asserts which rules may newly satisfy which win conditions.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: wakeEdges
fields.description: Rule-to-win-condition wake edges.
fields.specification: Each edge means the from rule may newly satisfy the to win condition.

fields.name: from_line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the waking rule.
fields.specification: Together with from_text, identifies the rule whose writes may affect a win condition.

fields.name: from_text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the waking rule.
fields.specification: Used with from_line to identify the source rule.

fields.name: to_line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the win condition.
fields.specification: Together with to_text, identifies the win condition that may become satisfied.

fields.name: to_text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the win condition.
fields.specification: Used with to_line to identify the destination win condition.

fields.name: reasons
fields.description: Why the win wake edge exists.
fields.specification: Allowed reasons are object_presence, object_absence, and movement.

fixtureSchemas.name: wincondition_tags
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each wincondition_tags fixture contains a winConditionTag array. Every row identifies one win condition by source line and exact source text, then asserts selected win-condition tags inside the row's tags object.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: winConditionTag
fields.description: Per-win-condition tag expectation rows.
fields.specification: Each row identifies exactly one analyzed win condition and contains a tags object with the win-condition tags this fixture wants to assert.

fields.name: line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the win condition.
fields.specification: Together with text, this must identify exactly one analyzed win condition.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the win condition.
fields.specification: Used with line to identify the win condition under test.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Win-condition tag expectations for this win condition.
fields.specification: Each field in tags is a documented win-condition tag and is compared with the derived win-condition tag value.

fields.name: objects_matched
fields.description: Concrete objects whose presence is read by this win condition.
fields.specification: For SOME and ALL this is subjects plus targets. For NO, only the targets mask contributes; the subject is an absence read and appears only in object_absences_matched.

fields.name: subjects_matched
fields.description: Concrete objects in the subjects mask when the quantifier is SOME or ALL.
fields.specification: When the quantifier is NO, the subjects represent an absence requirement, so subjects_matched is empty and the same concrete names appear in object_absences_matched.

fields.name: targets_matched
fields.description: Concrete objects in the targets mask.
fields.specification: Targets are the optional on X part of a win condition after resolving properties to concrete names. When there is no targets mask, targets_matched is empty.

fields.name: object_absences_matched
fields.description: Concrete objects whose absence is the win signal when the quantifier is NO.
fields.specification: For NO, the win condition is satisfied when none of the subject objects are present. For SOME and ALL, object_absences_matched is empty.

fixtureSchemas.name: mergeability
fixtureSchemas.description: Fixtures for same-layer object mergeability facts.
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each mergeability fixture contains mergePairs rows describing whether two same-layer objects can be merged without changing observable game behavior.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: mergePairs
fields.description: Mergeability expectations for object pairs.
fields.specification: Each row names a pair of same-layer objects and the status of the mergeability fact for that pair.

fields.name: objects
fields.description: Two object names that form the mergeability pair.
fields.specification: The runner sorts these names before matching the analyzed mergeability fact.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Whether the pair is a merge candidate or rejected.
fields.specification: candidate means the mergeability proof found no blockers. rejected means at least one blocker was found.

fields.name: blockers
fields.description: Reasons this pair is not mergeable.
fields.specification: Known blockers include individual_lhs_read, different_win_roles, and partial_property_observation.

fixtureSchemas.name: rulegroup_flow
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each rulegroup_flow fixture contains rulegroupFlow rows describing whether a plus-joined rule group can be split into independent sub-groups and, optionally, the exact dependency graph.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: rulegroupFlow
fields.description: Rule-group flow expectations.
fields.specification: Each row describes one source rule group containing more than one compiled rule.

fields.name: line
fields.description: 1-based source line where the group starts.
fields.specification: Used to identify the plus-joined rule group under test.

fields.name: split_candidate
fields.description: Whether this group can be split into independent sub-groups.
fields.specification: true when there are multiple connected components and no rulegroup-flow blockers.

fields.name: components_count
fields.description: Number of connected components in the group dependency graph.
fields.specification: A split candidate requires more than one component.

fields.name: blockers
fields.description: Reasons this rule group is not a split candidate.
fields.specification: Known blockers include single_component, random_rule_group, rigid_rule, and semantic_command.

fields.name: interactionEdges
fields.description: Optional exact intra-group dependency edges.
fields.specification: Each edge means one rule in the group may enable a later or earlier rule in the same group.

fields.name: from_line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the enabling rule.
fields.specification: Together with from_text, identifies the edge source rule.

fields.name: from_text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the enabling rule.
fields.specification: Used with from_line to identify the edge source rule.

fields.name: to_line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the enabled rule.
fields.specification: Together with to_text, identifies the edge destination rule.

fields.name: to_text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the enabled rule.
fields.specification: Used with to_line to identify the edge destination rule.

fields.name: reasons
fields.description: Why the interaction edge exists.
fields.specification: Allowed reasons are object_presence, object_absence, and movement.

fields.name: rerunMasks
fields.description: Optional exact earlier-rule rerun masks.
fields.specification: Each mask names a rule and the earlier rules in the same group that should be re-evaluated after it fires.

fields.name: line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the rule that may fire.
fields.specification: Together with text, identifies the rule whose rerun mask is listed.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the rule that may fire.
fields.specification: Used with line to identify the rule whose rerun mask is listed.

fields.name: rerun
fields.description: Earlier rules to re-evaluate after this rule fires.
fields.specification: Each row identifies one earlier rule in the same group.

fields.name: line
fields.description: 1-based source line of the earlier rule to re-evaluate.
fields.specification: Together with text, identifies a rerun target.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Trimmed source text of the earlier rule to re-evaluate.
fields.specification: Used with line to identify a rerun target.

fixtureSchemas.name: movement_action
fixtureSchemas.description: Fixtures for action-key movement reachability and solver no-op proofs.
fixtureSchemas.specification: Each movement_action fixture projects the report.facts.movement_action family into fields that are convenient to assert in testdata.

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Fixture format version.
fields.specification: Must be ps-static-analysis-testdata-v1.

fields.name: actionNoop
fields.description: Projected action_noop.value.
fields.specification: true only when pressing action has no solver-observable effect.

fields.name: actionNoopBlockers
fields.description: Projected action_noop.blockers.
fields.specification: Reasons the no-op proof was rejected. Known blockers are reads_action, queues_again, rigid_rule, autonomous_solver_active_rule, action_may_mutate_objects, and action_may_create_directional_movement.

fields.name: movements_reachable_from_action_input
fields.description: Projected movements_reachable_from_action_input.value.
fields.specification: Object:movement pairs reachable starting from player object action movement via a fixpoint over solver-active rules whose movement requirements are satisfiable. Runtime movement requirements are layer-based, so the check derives layer reachability from object movement facts instead of storing layer movement as the primary analysis state.
